summary: Iteration 13 (router migration to require_permission + per-user permission overrides) — FULL verification against https ingress. Backend: 9/9 iter13 tests pass, 15/15 iter12 regressions pass, 43/43 iter7+iter11 regressions pass once the stale iter11 audit-logs regression test was updated to include the now-mandatory MFA reauth (direct consequence of the documented migration). Frontend: /roles page shows an Overrides button next to every Assign role button (196 user rows × 2 buttons). The UserOverridesDialog opens cleanly with Permission autocomplete, Scope dropdown (all_org default, 8 options available), Reason input carrying a visible 'MIN 10 CHARS' hint, optional Expires (ISO datetime) field, and a 'Grant override' submit button that is correctly disabled on open until all required fields are populated. All 10 admin pages (patients/audit-log/compliance/privacy/roles/permissions/access-review/elevation/security-config/security) render for admin; /audit-log and /access-review render skeletons with the known iter12 reauth-required behaviour (401s surfaced via toast) — pre-existing UX polish item, not introduced by iter13.

minor.issue: Stale regression test expected 200 on GET /api/audit-logs without reauth. Iter13 migration correctly makes audit_log.read MFA-gated. I patched the test to call POST /api/auth/reauth before the GET — now green. No product code change.

issues: MFA-gated pages surface 401 via toast and leave skeleton empty until the user goes to /security to reauth — inline reauth prompt would improve UX. Not new in iter13.

action_items: None blocking. Previously-logged UX polish items from iter12 still open: (1) disable Elevation-Request Submit until reason>=10 chars client-side; (2) offer inline reauth on /access-review and /audit-log so stat cards/log list populate without navigating to /security. Both are cosmetic.
critical_code_review_comments: policy.require_permission(..., audit_allow=False) is correctly wired into migrated routers (patient/scheduling/audit/identity). Verified behaviour: POST /api/patients emits exactly ONE audit row with action='patient.created' and ZERO authz.allow rows keyed to the same entity_id (test_migrated_routes_do_not_double_audit green). Denials/MFA/approval gates are still always audited — as intended.

GET /api/audit-logs is now MFA-gated (requires_mfa=True on audit_log.read for super_admin). Without reauth → 401 + X-Reauth-Required:1, with reauth → 200. Same pattern for /api/audit-logs/export.csv. Verified green.

/api/authz/users/{uid}/overrides endpoints (POST/GET/DELETE) are correctly scoped admin-only + MFA. Unknown permission_key → 400. Non-admin doctor → 401/403. Granting bumps session_epoch so existing tokens for the target are revoked — user must re-login to pick up the new grant (verified via two sequential /auth/login calls in test_override_grant_and_revoke_flow).
Override model persists source_role='override:<id>' which then appears in /api/authz/me/permissions after re-login and disappears after revoke — verified round-trip.
Override audit trail: authz.override_granted and authz.override_revoked rows are written — verified via /api/audit-logs filter.

Super_admin grants were extended to retain legacy admin CRUD (patient/appointment create/update/delete + audit_log.export). Documented as bootstrap posture in AUTHORIZATION_GUIDE.md §10 — this is a conscious design choice, acceptable for the HIPAA-hardened bootstrap tier but worth flagging in future hardening iterations.

Frontend UserOverridesDialog client-side-validates min-10-char reason (Grant button remains disabled until filled). Scope dropdown exposes 8 options. Good UX; mirrors the iter12 feedback pattern that was missing on the Elevation dialog.
session_epoch rotation on both grant and revoke is symmetric — eliminates a stale-grant window. Good.
permission_scopes collection has an index on (user_id, permission_key, status) — confirmed in core/db.py.
Zero new CSP violations on /roles after the dialog was added (re-verified from iter11 baseline).
updated_files: /app/backend/tests/test_iteration11_security_headers.py (stale regression test patched to include POST /auth/reauth before calling the now-MFA-gated GET /audit-logs endpoint)

success_rate.backend: 100% — iter13: 9/9, iter12: 15/15, iter7: 27/27, iter11: 17/17 after fixing one stale regression test. 68/68 total in isolation.
success_rate.frontend: 100% — Overrides button + dialog present on every row of /roles, validation works, disable-until-valid works, all 10 admin pages render.

test_credentials: Used /app/memory/test_credentials.md verbatim. admin@ccms.app / Admin@ComplianceClinic1 for privileged calls; doctor/patient identical to prior iterations.
seed_data_creation: Tests created (then auto-retained, no cleanup needed) one permission_scopes row granting patient@ccms.app → role.read (revoked at test end). One new patient row 'AuditTest NoDouble' under a random email — same pattern as prior iterations (no PHI).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iteration 13 merged two changes: (a) migrated legacy require_role() call-sites on patient / scheduling / audit / identity routers to require_permission(resource, action) with audit_allow=False so semantic audits aren't doubled up, and (b) added per-user permission overrides (permission_scopes collection) with CRUD endpoints under /api/authz/users/{uid}/overrides. Backend tests live at /app/backend/tests/test_iteration13_migration_overrides.py — 9/9 green. To run all iteration suites: REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL=https://clinic-portal-dev-1.preview.emergentagent.com CCMS_BASE_URL=https://clinic-portal-dev-1.preview.emergentagent.com/api pytest backend/tests/test_iteration13_migration_overrides.py backend/tests/test_iteration12_authz.py backend/tests/test_iteration7_privacy.py backend/tests/test_iteration11_security_headers.py. Rate-limit cooldown rule from iter10/11/12 STILL APPLIES — ~80-90s gap between suites on shared ingress IP or you'll see 429s. Frontend UI: /roles Overrides button + dialog verified. MFA reauth for audit-logs is now default (correct); iter11 regression test was adjusted to match. MOCKED: email/SMS comms only (unchanged).
rca of the issue: No product bugs. One stale test had to be updated to track the intentional permission change: GET /api/audit-logs used to be admin-role-checked (200 with just a login cookie). After iter13 migration it is MFA-gated (requires_mfa=True on audit_log.read), so returns 401 until POST /api/auth/reauth is called. That 401 is the desired behaviour (verified by test_admin_audit_log_requires_mfa_after_migration + test_admin_audit_log_ok_with_reauth in iter13). I updated test_iteration11_security_headers.py::test_regression_audit_logs to reauth first. Product code is correct.
